description: 'whelina' is grass as a plant kind or the ground cover formed by many grass plants. Narrow leaves and jointed stems are familiar; a lawn and a short height are only two possible forms. 'pholui' (bamboo) and many grains belong to the wider grass family while keeping their own useful names.
articulatory_notes: Rounded lips shape unvoiced 'wh' and let breath pass into front 'e'. Stressed lateral 'li' brings the tongue to the alveolar ridge; dental nasal 'n' then opens into final 'a'.
usage_notes: The noun often behaves as a mass term for ground cover. An individual blade or plant can be identified by context, and a counted living plant may take optional classifier 'lipha'.

semantic_domains.nature: grass plants or the ground cover they form
semantic_domains.physical: a low plant cover made from many narrow leaves and stems
